overview: The PLI Scheme for Specialty Steel is a Production Linked Incentive scheme notified by the Ministry of Steel, Government of India, to promote domestic manufacturing of specialty steel grades and enhance technology and value chain positioning of the Indian steel industry. The scheme provides financial incentives based on incremental production of eligible specialty steel products over a five-year period, with incentives disbursed from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31. It targets five broad product categories: Coated/Plated Steel Products, High Strength/Wear resistant Steel, Specialty Rails, Alloy Steel Products and Steel wires, and Electrical Steel (CRGO, CRNO).
objectives: Promote manufacturing of specialty steel grades within the country
Help the Indian steel industry mature in terms of technology
Move up the value chain in the steel industry
Incentivize incremental production of eligible specialty steel products
Support capacity addition and augmentation through new and existing facilities
eligibility: Companies registered in India under the Companies Act 2013, desirous of manufacturing identified specialty steel grades, shall be eligible to apply for incentive under the scheme. The company shall ensure end-to-end manufacturing of applied product sub-category domestically, where the input material is melted and poured within the country using iron ore/scrap/sponge iron/pellets, etc. The net worth of the company (including that of the group companies) shall not be less than 30% of the total committed investment. Third party outsourcing is allowed up to 20% of value addition. For capacity augmentation, 50% of the threshold investment shall be applicable.
benefits: Financial incentives linked to incremental production of specialty steel products, calculated as (Eligible incremental production) x (Weighted Average Sales Price in base year or current year, whichever is lower) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100. Incentive disbursement is capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company per annum across all product categories. Non-financial benefits include eligibility to avail benefits under other schemes such as RoDTEP or State Government schemes, and the ability to undertake third-party value addition up to 20%.
financial_support: Incentive is calculated based on incremental production of applied product sub-category using the formula: Incentive = (A) x (B or C whichever is lowest) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100, where A = Eligible incremental production derived from sales, B = Weighted Average sales price of the applicant in current year, C = Weighted Average sales price in base year (2019-20). The PLI rate varies by slab (A, B, C) and year, ranging from 3% to 15%. The annual incentive payable is capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company. Incentive disbursement begins from FY 2026-27 for the production period FY 2025-26 to FY 2029-30.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 200 crores per annum per eligible company
application_process: 1. Register on the PLI web portal (https://plimos.mecon.co.in). 2. Fill the application form online within 30 days from the date of launch of PLI Scheme 1.2. 3. Pay a non-refundable application fee of Rs 1 lakh (plus applicable GST). 4. Submit the signed copy of the online application along with all uploaded documents within 10 days of online submission. 5. Undergo application scrutiny by PMA for completeness of supporting documents. 6. A one-time correction window of 15 days is available after closure of application submission for completeness of supporting documents only. 7. Selected companies sign a MoU with the Ministry of Steel. 8. Submit performance security of 0.5% of committed investment in the form of Bank Guarantee. 9. Submit undertaking consenting to audit of manufacturing facilities. 10. File annual incentive claims within 7 months from the end of the financial year to which the claim pertains.
required_documents: Details of source of fund (Equity and Debt)
Undertaking on value addition through third parties
deadlines: Online Registration and Application Submission dates: Start Date: 04.11.2025, End Date: To be announced (based on PLI Scheme 1.2 launch). Correction window: A one-time correction window of fifteen days (15 days) shall be notified after close of application submission and evaluation by PMA.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Steel, Government of India
contact_details: Nodal Officer from PMA: Senior General Manager, Rolling Mills, & Nodal Officer PLI Scheme for Specialty steel, MECON Limited, Vivekanand Path, Doranda, Ranchi-834002, e-mail: plischeme@meconlimited.co.in; Nodal Officer from MoS: Deputy Secretary, Steel, Ministry of Steel (MoS), Govt. of India, Udyog Bhavan, New Delhi – 110011, e-mail: pli-mosteel@gov.in
caveats: Incentive disbursement is limited to the budgeted outlay and capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company per annum.
The scheme period is for a maximum of five years, with incentive disbursement from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31.
In case of deferment by up to two years for specific product categories, the incentive release will be from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31 (or FY 2027-28 to FY 2031-32 if deferred).
The actual permissible investment must be more than the limiting investment for a product sub-category to be eligible for incentive.
Performance security of 0.5% of committed investment must be submitted and is valid throughout the scheme period.
Claims for disbursement of incentive must be filed within seven months from the end of the financial year to which the claim pertains.
Investments made before the date of notification of the scheme (04.11.2025) are not eligible under permissible investment.
The selected company must enter into a separate MoU with the Ministry of Steel for each product sub-category applied for.
If an applicant fails to meet committed production for two consecutive years, the performance security may be invoked.
The MoS/PMA has the right to verify information and direct refund of incentives with interest in case of misrepresentation or fraud.
